item.title: Prime Minister Marape welcomes restoration of direct Air Niugini flights to Japan
item.description: Prime Minister James Marape described the successful restoration of Air Niugini’s direct flights between Port Moresby and Narita International Airport in Japan, as a major milestone in PNG’s Aviation Sector. He also welcomed this restoration as a significant step forward in strengthening the country’s economic, tourism, investment and people-to-people ties with one of its closest and longest-stand
item.content:encoded:
Prime Minister James Marape described the successful restoration of Air Niugini’s direct flights between Port Moresby and Narita International Airport in Japan, as a major milestone in PNG’s Aviation Sector.

He also welcomed this restoration as a significant step forward in strengthening the country’s economic, tourism, investment and people-to-people ties with one of its closest and longest-standing development partners.

The inaugural restored service departed Jacksons International Airport last week as Flight PX54 and landed safely at Narita International Airport after approximately six hours, marking the return of direct air connectivity between Papua New Guinea and Japan after several years.

Prime Minister Marape congratulated the staff of Air Niugini and all stakeholders whose commitment made the restoration of the important international route possible.

The Prime Minister noted that Japan has remained one of Papua New Guinea’s most trusted development and economic partners for decades, contributing significantly to infrastructure development, fisheries, energy, agriculture, health, education and human resource development.

“Japan has stood beside Papua New Guinea throughout our development journey.

“Modern aviation is one of the engines of economic growth. Every new international route creates opportunities for investment, employment, exports, tourism and commerce. Improved air connectivity reduces barriers to business and positions Papua New Guinea more competitively within the Asia-Pacific region.” PM Marape said.

Prime Minister Marape said the restoration of the Narita service aligns with the Government’s long-term vision of expanding Papua New Guinea’s international aviation network.

“We are also exploring new opportunities with nations such as the United Arab Emirates and elsewhere to establish additional direct international connections that will strengthen Papua New Guinea’s place in the global economy.

“The expansion of Jacksons International Airport demonstrates our confidence that Papua New Guinea will become an increasingly important Aviation Hub for the Pacific Region.” Mr. Marape said.

He encouraged Papua New Guineans to take advantage of the renewed service to strengthen business relationships, educational opportunities and tourism exchanges with Japan while inviting more Japanese travelers to discover Papua New Guinea.

item.title: NMSA warns boat owners to follow sea safety laws
item.description: The National Maritime Safety Authority [NMSA] will not hesitate to arrest and prosecute small boat owners and operators who do not comply to the Small Craft Act (SCA) 2011. NMSA Chief Executive Officer, Pawa Limu issued this warning in light of the recent boat incidents in East New Britain and New Ireland provinces and said, small boat owners, operators and passengers in the maritime provinces mus
item.content:encoded:
The National Maritime Safety Authority [NMSA] will not hesitate to arrest and prosecute small boat owners and operators who do not comply to the Small Craft Act (SCA) 2011.

NMSA Chief Executive Officer, Pawa Limu issued this warning in light of the recent boat incidents in East New Britain and New Ireland provinces and said, small boat owners, operators and passengers in the maritime provinces must exercise responsibility and comply with sea safety measures.

Communities in the 15 maritime communities who use the sea for their daily sustenance have been warned to check local weather report, inform others of where you are going, always wear life jacket, ensure to take extra food and drinking water, make sure the outboard motor is working properly, not to get on boat with drunkards and overload passengers.

The NMSA reminded provincial governments of the 15 maritime provinces to ensure a high-level maritime safety is enforced on people who rely on the sea for livelihood.

Mr Limu said the maritime provinces must take full responsibility to ensure SCA 2011 is effectively implemented to improve sea safety for owners and operators.

“Under the law for practical implementation and enforcement, each maritime province has different circumstances and challenges that require local solutions,” Limu said.

Mr Limu warned all boat owners, operators and passengers that anyone caught breaching the Small Craft Act will face the consequences of the law.

The NMSA Maritime Rescue Coordinating Centre [MRCC] is on standby to assist all distresses in any emergency.

MRCC can be contacted on toll free number 73517017 or email address mrcc@nmsa.gov.pg

item.title: Strong wind forces search to be stopped
item.description: The search for five missing public servants and their family members was suspended at 4pm today due to strong winds and rough sea conditions. Chairman of the Provincial Disaster Committee, Levi Mano, said sea searches had already been suspended yesterday, while the aerial search that began this morning was also called off because of the weather.
item.content:encoded:
The search for five missing public servants and their family members was suspended at 4pm today due to strong winds and rough sea conditions.

Chairman of the Provincial Disaster Committee, Levi Mano, said sea searches had already been suspended yesterday, while the aerial search that began this morning was also called off because of the weather.

Mr Mano said the aerial search team located one of the two missing boats off the coast of Messi, a coastal village in Namatanai, Central New Ireland. However, there was no sign of the passengers.

item.title: New education minister sets priorities
item.description: The new Minister for Education and Member for Kerowagi, Francis Kikin Siune, has thanked Prime Minister James Marape for his new appointment and has promised to deliver on the country's shared vision. Minister Kikin said his priorities include improving foundation literacy and numeracy, enhancing teacher quality, expanding second chance education, accelerating digital transformation in education,
item.content:encoded:
The new Minister for Education and Member for Kerowagi, Francis Kikin Siune, has thanked Prime Minister James Marape for his new appointment and has promised to deliver on the country's shared vision.

Minister Kikin said his priorities include improving foundation literacy and numeracy, enhancing teacher quality, expanding second chance education, accelerating digital transformation in education, and expanding equitable access to education.

"Education is a shared responsibility. We must ensure that every child starts school and completes Grade 12.

"Every student has the opportunity to succeed, and every citizen who has missed out on formal education must be given a second chance to realise their full potential," Minister Kikin said.

He warned that school heads and boards must be accountable to their schools.

He also stressed that every kina spent, every hour allocated, and every resource deployed must directly serve the goal of creating capable, critical-thinking and well-rounded students.

"I will work closely with all our partners and stakeholders to build an education system that leaves no one behind," Mr Kikin said.

item.title: Joseph Manu arrives in PNG for Chiefs visit
item.description: Former Sydney Rooster and New Zealand Kiwi International Joseph Manu arrived in Port Moresby this afternoon with his family, accompanied by PNG Chiefs General Manager for Football Michael Chammas and CEO Lorna McPherson. It was reported earlier this year in April that the new NRL Franchise, PNG Chiefs were after Manu's signature, and his visit to Port Moresby today to see the facilities is likely
item.content:encoded:
Former Sydney Rooster and New Zealand Kiwi International Joseph Manu arrived in Port Moresby this afternoon with his family, accompanied by PNG Chiefs General Manager for Football Michael Chammas and CEO Lorna McPherson.

It was reported earlier this year in April that the new NRL Franchise, PNG Chiefs were after Manu's signature, and his visit to Port Moresby today to see the facilities is likely to see the Kiwi return to rugby league after moving to Japan to play rugby union.

PNG Chiefs General Manager for Football Michael Chammas told media at the Jackson International Airport that Manu's visit is mainly for him and his family to see the facilities and if all goes well, the two time NRL premiership winner could be the next marque signing for the Chiefs.

Manu's visit also indicates that the PNG Chiefs are serious in building a solid roster when they enter the NRL in 2028 with the signings of Jerome Luai, Alex Johnston, Connor Watson and Matty Lees locked in earlier.

Chammas made it clear that the Chiefs mean business in bringing together the games elite talent in order for the Chiefs to be competitive in their inaugural year.

PNG born talents in Gairo Voro, Morea Morea and Finley Glare also signed development contracts recently with promise to improve their contracts if they continue to perform consistently in the UK [London Broncos].

item.title: PNG urged to embrace AI and digital technology
item.description: Prime Minister James Marape says Papua New Guinea and the Pacific must not be left behind as the world moves rapidly towards artificial intelligence [AI] and digital technology. Speaking at the opening of the Digital Transformation Summit 2026 in Port Moresby today, the Prime Minister said information and communication technology [ICT] is now a key driver of economic growth, education, health and
item.content:encoded:
Prime Minister James Marape says Papua New Guinea and the Pacific must not be left behind as the world moves rapidly towards artificial intelligence [AI] and digital technology.

Speaking at the opening of the Digital Transformation Summit 2026 in Port Moresby today, the Prime Minister said information and communication technology [ICT] is now a key driver of economic growth, education, health and government services.

"AI has leapfrogged almost all other ICT conversations and AI is taking dominance. We must not be left behind," Mr Marape said.

Mr Marape said Papua New Guinea has made significant progress in expanding internet access, with broadband coverage increasing from 40 percent in 2020 to 80 percent this year.

"We have made some progress in this space. And we have also seen good and the bad side about this. Papua New Guinea, to give you all some context, is not a nation of one or two or three islands.

The Prime Minister said the government is working to elevate ICT as a central government function to strengthen digital services and support the country's development.

"ICT will be elevated to the central government agencies. We want to make sure it protects and develops our country," he said.

The Prime Minister also warned that greater digital connectivity has brought new security risks.

"Papua New Guinea is now witnessing almost an 80 percent increase in cyber threats," he said.

Mr Marape called on Pacific leaders who are also at this summit to work together on ICT policies that improve digital connectivity while protecting people, businesses and government systems from cybercrime.

He said the Pacific should develop a common approach to ICT that supports economic growth, improves services and strengthens regional cooperation.

The summit is hosted by the Department of Information and Communications Technology [DICT].

item.title: Air search steps up for missing families at sea
item.description: The search for five public servants and their family members missing at sea has stepped up today, with a helicopter joining the rescue operation. A Manalos Aviation helicopter from Lae was brought in by the East New Britain Provincial Disaster Office to assist in the search, which has now entered its second day.
item.content:encoded:
The search for five public servants and their family members missing at sea has stepped up today, with a helicopter joining the rescue operation.

A Manalos Aviation helicopter from Lae was brought in by the East New Britain Provincial Disaster Office to assist in the search, which has now entered its second day.

The helicopter arrived at Tokua Airport this morning to refuel and finalise flight plans before beginning an aerial search along the St George Channel between East New Britain and New Ireland provinces.

Authorities say those missing, include 17 children and nine adults.

The group was reportedly returning from a family outing at Pangpang Island at around 4pm on Saturday when they encountered rough seas.

Initial reports indicate the two boats were hit by strong winds and large waves, causing them to capsize.

The two boat operators were rescued on Sunday, while the search continues for the remaining passengers.

East New Britain Governor Michael Marum is expected to hold a media conference at 3pm today to provide an update on the search operation.

item.title: Search continues for five ENB PHA staff and families missing at sea
item.description: The search for five East New Britain Provincial Health Authority [ENB PHA] senior staff and their family members reported missing at sea between Pidgin Island and Kokopo has been scaled down overnight, with authorities to resume operations at first light. The missing people have been identified as ENB PHA staff members Carol Malisa, Clive Pennie, Manuel Sialis and Raymond Maramun, along with their
item.content:encoded:
The search for five East New Britain Provincial Health Authority [ENB PHA] senior staff and their family members reported missing at sea between Pidgin Island and Kokopo has been scaled down overnight, with authorities to resume operations at first light.

The missing people have been identified as ENB PHA staff members Carol Malisa, Clive Pennie, Manuel Sialis and Raymond Maramun, along with their family members. Carol Malisa and Clive Pennie are a couple, while Relvie Taplar is also among those reported missing.

ENB PHA Chief Executive Officer Dr Ako Yap confirmed that the staff members and their families went missing late Saturday afternoon while travelling between Pidgin Island and Kokopo.

East New Britain Provincial Police Commander Chief Superintendent Amanda Kates said search teams had spent the day searching the waters using six banana boats, the Police Patrol Vessel [PPV] Manning and a Royal Papua New Guinea Constabulary boat.

"We have searched long and hard today. We've had six banana boats out from all the way down from Rabaul up to here," Chief Superintedent Kates said.

"Unfortunately, we have not found any debris or anything at all that would indicate that at this stage there is something to give us a little bit of hope."

She said the search was called off as darkness approached due to strong winds and difficult sea conditions.

"It's coming on to the hours of darkness and it's getting strong winds, so we're going to call the search off today. But tomorrow morning we're going to do a low-key search," she said.

Chief Superintendent Kates said while the chances of finding survivors were becoming lower, authorities were not giving up hope.

"The likelihood that we're going to find survivors is low, but we are spreading our thoughts and our prayers. You never know, it may have got to New Ireland. There is always hope," she said.

She said police in New Ireland had also been alerted to assist with the search, while fishing vessels operating in the area had been asked to keep watch for any signs of the missing people.

"We've put a call out to all the fishing vessels to actually keep searching. Even though we have stopped searching, everybody else is still searching and we're going to try and find something to give you closure or to give you hope," Chief Superintendent Kates said.

The search is expected to continue at first light with the PPV Manning and RPNGC boats returning to the sea.

item.title: Bando accepts move to Immigration Ministry
item.description: Former Coffee Minister William Bando says he respects Prime Minister James Marape's decision to appoint him as the new Minister for Immigration following the recent Cabinet reshuffle.

item.title: 1 killed, 4 hospitalised after alleged sorcery attack in Karamui
item.description: One man is dead, and four others are hospitalized after a violent attack in Wenaiyo Village, Karamui-Nomane district, Simbu province, over allegations of sorcery.
